chunks.text: 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Bail Application No.S-463 of 2021 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE. For hearing of bail application. 23.09.2021 Mr. Iftikhar Ali Bhatti, Advocate for applicant. Syed Sardar Ali Shah, D.P.G for the State. *************** O R D E R Mr. Abdul Rahim Jamro Advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of complainant, which is taken on record. Through instant Crl. Bail application, applicant Shafique Ahmed son of Ghulam Qadir seeks pre-arrest bail in Crime No.67 of 2021 registered at Police Station, Sobhodero under section 489-F, 506/2 P.P.C. The applicant was admitted to interim pre-arrest bail by this Court subject to furnishing of solvent surety in the sum of Rs.300,000/- and PR bond in the like amount vide order, dated 29.07.2021, now he seeks confirmation of the same. Briefly stated facts of the case are that on 19.04.2021, complainant Muhammad Ayoob lodged the aforementioned F.I.R. alleging therein that applicant/accused received some amount from complainant in lieu of providing Government job to his son, since he failed to do so, he issued for repayment of said amount cheques of different dates of MCB Branch, Sobhodero, which were dishonored on presentation of the same. At the very outset, learned counsel for the complainant states that he has no objection for confirmation of interim pre-arrest bail subject to raising the bail amount from Rs.300,000/- to Rs.11,80,000/- by the applicant, equivalent to cheques amount. 2 Learned Counsel for applicant/accused while showing his willingness for furnishing additional surety of Rs.8,80,000/- requests for the grant of two week’stime for furnishing additional surety.

Briefly stated facts of the case are that on 19.04.2021, complainant Muhammad Ayoob lodged the aforementioned F.I.R. alleging therein that applicant/accused received some amount from complainant in lieu of providing Government job to his son, since he failed to do so, he issued for repayment of said amount cheques of different dates of MCB Branch, Sobhodero, which were dishonored on presentation of the same. At the very outset, learned counsel for the complainant states that he has no objection for confirmation of interim pre-arrest bail subject to raising the bail amount from Rs.300,000/- to Rs.11,80,000/- by the applicant, equivalent to cheques amount. 2 Learned Counsel for applicant/accused while showing his willingness for furnishing additional surety of Rs.8,80,000/- requests for the grant of two week’stime for furnishing additional surety. Accordingly, the interim bail granted to applicant vide order dated 29.07.2021 is hereby confirmed by consent subject to his furnishing additional surety of Rs.8,80,000/- with fresh PR bond of Rs. 11,80,000/- to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court within one weeks hereof. However, in case applicant fails to furnish additional surety within stipulated period, this Crl. Bail Application shall stands dismissed by recalling interim order dated 29.07.2021. Application stands disposed of in above terms. J U D G E Ihsan/*
